trade.summary: Japan is China's third-largest trading partner and China is Japan's largest. Both are deeply economically intertwined despite political tensions. Japan exports vehicles, machinery, electronics, and chemicals. China exports electronics, clothing, machinery, and chemicals. Japan is pursuing supply chain diversification but deep interdependence persists.
disputes: Japan's semiconductor export controls aligned with US
Fukushima water release controversy (China banned Japanese seafood)
Rare earth export restrictions by China (2010, recurring)

diplomatic.summary: Relations are characterized by deep structural tensions despite economic interdependence. The Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands dispute remains unresolved. Japan has aligned closely with US on China policy, joining export controls on semiconductors and increasing defense spending. China has imposed seafood bans in retaliation for Fukushima water release. Historical memories of WWII periodically inflame public opinion.
recent_incidents: China imposed ban on Japanese seafood imports (Fukushima wastewater, 2023)
Japan joined US-led semiconductor export controls on China
Chinese military aircraft violations of Japanese ADIZ (recurring)
Japan's new National Security Strategy designates China as top security challenge (2022)
Chinese coast guard confrontations near Senkaku Islands (recurring)

military.summary: Significant military tension. China's frequent military exercises near Taiwan, ADIZ intrusions, and assertive posture in East China Sea are primary drivers of Japan's historic defense buildup. Japan has increased defense spending to 2% GDP and acquired counterstrike capabilities. Japan-US alliance provides extended deterrence. No arms transfers. Near-miss incidents recurring.
incidents: Multiple Chinese military aircraft in Japanese ADIZ (2023-2025)
Japanese coast guard confrontation with Chinese survey vessels
Chinese warship transiting through Japan's Osumi Strait

financial.summary: Deep financial interdependence but declining. Japanese companies (Toyota, Sony, Honda) are large investors in China. China holds significant Japanese yen-denominated assets. Japan is diversifying away from China, incentivizing factory relocations. Chinese investment in Japan is limited due to Japan's FDI screening.

energy.summary: Japan imports Chinese solar panels and batteries. China imports some Japanese LNG and processed energy products. Japan sources some Chinese coal and minerals. Japan is reducing rare earth dependency on China following past export restrictions.

scientific.summary: Historically significant scientific cooperation now under severe scrutiny. Japan has implemented technology transfer controls aligned with US export control frameworks. Joint publications are declining as Japanese universities review Chinese partnerships for dual-use concerns.
